title: Built-in React hooks (overview)
summary: React ships built-in hooks for state, effects, context, performance, concurrent features, and (in React 19) forms and reading promises/context with use().
content:
## Categories

- **State:** `useState`, `useReducer`, `useActionState` (19), `useOptimistic` (19)
- **Context:** `useContext`, `use` (19) for context
- **Ref:** `useRef`, `useImperativeHandle`
- **Effects:** `useEffect`, `useLayoutEffect`, `useInsertionEffect`
- **Performance:** `useMemo`, `useCallback`, `useDeferredValue`, `useTransition`, `use` (19)
- **External stores / SSR:** `useSyncExternalStore`, `useId`
- **Forms (19):** `useFormStatus`
- **Debug:** `useDebugValue`

## Custom hooks

Name any function `useX` that calls other hooks to share stateful logic.

## Server vs client

Most hooks run in Client Components. `use` can run in Server Components to await promises in React 19—follow your framework rules.

code_examples.title: Import from 'react'
code_examples.code:
import {
  useState,
  useEffect,
  useContext,
  useReducer,
  useCallback,
  useMemo,
  useRef,
  useImperativeHandle,
  useLayoutEffect,
  useDebugValue,
  useDeferredValue,
  useTransition,
  useId,
  useSyncExternalStore,
  useInsertionEffect,
} from 'react';
// React 19: use, useActionState, useFormStatus, useOptimistic

title: useState
summary: useState adds local state to function components. Calling the setter schedules a re-render with the next state value.
content:
## Signature

`const [state, setState] = useState(initial)`

## Functional updates

When the next state depends on the previous one, pass an updater: `setCount((c) => c + 1)` to avoid stale closures in rapid updates.

## Batching

React batches state updates in event handlers. Multiple `setState` calls may coalesce into one render.

code_examples.title: Counter

title: useEffect
summary: useEffect runs side effects after paint. The dependency array controls when the effect re-runs.
content:
## Dependencies

- **[]** — run once after mount (and cleanup on unmount).
- **[a, b]** — run when `a` or `b` change.
- **omit array** — run after every render (usually avoid).

## Cleanup

Return a function from the effect to unsubscribe timers, listeners, or abort fetches.

code_examples.title: Subscribe with cleanup

title: useContext
summary: useContext reads the nearest matching Context.Provider value. Use for dependency injection, themes, and rarely-changing shared data—not as a full Redux replacement for huge trees.
content:
## Pattern

Create context with `createContext(default)`. Wrap subtree with `<Provider value={...}>`. Children call `useContext(MyContext)`.

## Re-renders

When `value` identity changes, every consumer re-renders. Split contexts or memoize values to avoid churn.

## vs Redux

Context triggers broad updates without selectors; Redux adds predictable updates, middleware, DevTools, and normalized patterns.

title: useReducer
summary: useReducer is like useState but updates go through a reducer `(state, action) => newState`—ideal for multi-step flows and predictable transitions.
content:
## Signature

`const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, initialArg, init?)`

## Lazy init

Pass `init` as third argument with factory `(initialArg) => state` for expensive initial state.

## When to prefer over useState

Multiple related sub-values, undo/redo, or updates that are easier to express as actions.

title: useCallback
summary: useCallback returns a memoized function reference. Use it to stabilize callbacks passed to memoized children or as effect dependencies.
content:
## Signature

`const fn = useCallback(fn, deps)`

## Mind the deps

If dependencies change, you get a new function—same as inline handlers.

## When it helps

If `Child` is wrapped in `React.memo`, passing stable callbacks avoids useless child renders.

title: useMemo
summary: useMemo caches a computed value until dependencies change—use for expensive pure calculations, not for every render.
content:
## Signature

`const value = useMemo(() => compute(a, b), [a, b])`

## Pitfalls

Reference equality: returning new objects/arrays each time defeats memoization downstream.

## Rule of thumb

Measure first; avoid premature optimization.

code_examples.code: const sorted = useMemo(() => items.slice().sort((x, y) => x.date - y.date), [items]);

title: useRef
summary: useRef holds a mutable `.current` box that persists across renders without triggering re-renders when updated.
content:
## DOM refs

`<div ref={ref} />` assigns the element to `ref.current`.

## Instance fields

Store timers, latest props, or any mutable value that shouldn’t cause re-render on write.

## vs useState

Updating `ref.current` does not schedule a render.

title: useImperativeHandle
summary: useImperativeHandle customizes the instance value exposed when parent uses ref—prefer declarative props when possible.
content:
## Usage

Pair with `forwardRef`. Factory builds the object parents see on `ref.current`.

## Style

React encourages data flowing down; imperative handles are for rare cases (focus, animation controllers, maps).

title: useLayoutEffect
summary: Fires synchronously after DOM mutations but before paint—use for measuring layout or applying DOM writes that must happen before the user sees a frame.
content:
## vs useEffect

useEffect defers to after paint; useLayoutEffect blocks paint until complete—keep work tiny.

## SSR note

Warnings if DOM assumptions don’t match server; sometimes split logic.

title: useDebugValue
summary: useDebugValue adds labels for custom hooks in React DevTools—does nothing in production builds.
content:
## Signature

`useDebugValue(value, format?)`

## Usage

Inside custom hooks to show concise state snapshots while debugging.

title: useDeferredValue
summary: Returns a deferred version of a value that may lag behind—for keeping inputs responsive while expensive UI catches up.
content:
## Pattern

Pass fast-updating state as input; render heavy subtree from deferred copy so React can interrupt lower priority work.

## vs startTransition

`useTransition` marks updates as transitions explicitly; `useDeferredValue` derives a lagging copy of some prop/state.

code_examples.code:
const deferredQuery = useDeferredValue(query);
const results = useMemo(() => search(deferredQuery), [deferredQuery]);

title: useTransition
summary: Marks state updates as non-urgent transitions and exposes `isPending` while the transition is in flight.
content:
## Signature

`const [isPending, startTransition] = useTransition()`

## Usage

Wrap `setState` calls that should not block urgent UI (typing, clicking).

title: useId
summary: Generates unique stable IDs across SSR and client—use for linking labels, aria attributes, and SVG defs.
content:
## vs incremental counters

Avoid `useRef(++id)`—hydration mismatch risk.

## Prefix

`const id = useId();` returns colon-containing strings safe for HTML.

title: useSyncExternalStore
summary: Subscribe to an external data source safely under concurrent rendering—foundation for state libraries and bridging non-React stores.
content:
## API

`useSyncExternalStore(subscribe, getSnapshot, getServerSnapshot?)`

## Rules

`getSnapshot` must be cached per store state version; server snapshot avoids hydration mismatch.

title: useInsertionEffect
summary: Fires before layout effects, meant for injecting styles into the DOM—primarily used by CSS-in-JS libraries.
content:
## Timing

Before `useLayoutEffect`, after DOM mutation—avoid general app logic here unless building a styling runtime.

title: use() — read Context & Promises (React 19)
summary: The `use` hook lets you read a Promise or Context during render—integrates with Suspense for async data.
content:
## Promises

`const data = use(promise)` suspends until resolved (needs Suspense boundary).

## Context

`const theme = use(ThemeContext)`—can be called conditionally unlike `useContext`.

## Caveats

Follow your bundler/framework rules for Server vs Client usage.

title: useActionState (React 19)
summary: Wraps async actions for forms: gives pending state and last returned value—pairs well with Server Actions.
content:
## Pattern

`const [state, submitAction, isPending] = useActionState(fn, initialState)`

## Forms

`<form action={submitAction}>` progressive enhancement story.

title: useFormStatus (React 19)
summary: Must be called from a component rendered inside a `<form>`—exposes pending state of the parent form submission.
content:
## Constraint

Only works under a form subtree so React can associate status with the right form.

## UX

Disable buttons or show spinners during submission.

title: useOptimistic (React 19)
summary: Shows an optimistic UI layer while an async action completes; reverts when the real state lands.
content:
## Signature

`const [optimistic, addOptimistic] = useOptimistic(state, reducer)`

## Use cases

Likes, messages, toggles—pair with transitions or server reconciliation.
